name: Dominica
avoid: July to November is wetter and overlaps the core Atlantic hurricane season, so Boiling Lake, Titou Gorge, whale-watching departures, and northeast-coast drives are more likely to be disrupted.
Cruise-ship call days make central Roseau busier, so Fort Young waterfront, Old Market lanes, and taxi ranks feel more crowded for a few daytime hours.
Dominica's mountain roads are narrow, steep, and slow even in good weather, so avoid over-packing the route if you are self-driving between Roseau Valley, Portsmouth, and Calibishie.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, stay in Roseau or the Laudat / Wotten Waven area, buy the Morne Trois Pitons day or week pass, and handle Portsmouth plus Calibishie as long day trips or skip one coast entirely.

cities.name: Roseau
cities.notes: The capital is the easiest first base for ferry arrivals, whale-watching departures, Roseau market energy, and day trips into the national-park interior.

cities.name: Morne Trois Pitons
cities.notes: Use the Laudat–Trafalgar–Wotten Waven highlands as your rainforest lodge base for Boiling Lake, Titou Gorge, Emerald Pool, hot springs, and Dominica's signature interior hikes.

cities.name: Portsmouth
cities.notes: Dominica's north-west hub works well for Cabrits National Park, Fort Shirley, Purple Turtle Beach, Syndicate, and easier access to the island's second town services.

cities.name: Calibishie
cities.notes: The quiet northeast coast is the scenic finish for Red Rocks, Batibou-style beaches, village guesthouses, and short drives to Chaudiere Pool and Pointe Baptiste.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Dominica and FLOW sell the most practical prepaid data; budget about ₹900–₹1,800 for a short-stay pack and download offline maps before hiking days because interior coverage fades around trailheads.

Roseau.name: Roseau
Roseau.note: Roseau works best at the start or end of the trip because shared minibuses, ferry logistics, dive shops, and whale-watch departures are easiest to coordinate here.

activities.name: Dominica Botanic Gardens and Sisserou parrot enclosure walk
Old Market Plaza, Bayfront, and Roseau Cathedral stroll
Morne Bruce viewpoint
Dominica Museum stop

days.theme: Whale Watching & Roseau Waterfront Slow Day

activities.name: Dive Dominica or PH Whale Watch Dominica shared whale-watching cruise
activities.tip: Shared departures are commonly published around US$69–85 per adult; Dominica is one of the few places where resident sperm whales are seen year-round, though February to April usually brings calmer seas.

activities.name: Fort Young waterfront and cruise pier walk
Roseau Public Market for spices, bay oil, and cocoa tea
Sunset dinner by the bayfront

days.theme: Easy South-West Day for Sulphur Springs & Snorkel Time

activities.name: Soufriere Sulphur Springs single-site pass
activities.tip: The Forestry eco-site single-visit pass is officially listed at US$8 / EC$21.74 for one fee-controlled site.

activities.name: Champagne Reef shore snorkel
Scott's Head viewpoint walk
Roseau evening return by minibus or taxi
activities.cost: ₹110–₹310 pp by bus; ₹1,400–₹2,500 taxi depending on distance

Morne Trois Pitons.name: Morne Trois Pitons
Morne Trois Pitons.note: Laudat and Trafalgar are the most practical sleep bases if Boiling Lake is the priority; buy the day pass for multi-stop waterfall days or the week pass if you will split hikes across several days.

days.theme: Trafalgar Falls, Titou Gorge & Wotten Waven Soak

activities.name: Morne Trois Pitons eco-site day pass covering Trafalgar Falls and Titou Gorge
activities.tip: Dominica Forestry lists the all-sites day pass at US$20 / EC$54.39, which is the best-value option if you combine multiple fee-controlled nature stops in one day.

activities.name: Trafalgar Falls single-site visit
activities.tip: If you only do Trafalgar Falls, widely published on-site pricing is about EC$14, roughly ₹435 per adult.

activities.name: Titou Gorge swim-through canyon (included with the pass)
Wotten Waven hot-spring soak

days.theme: Boiling Lake Hike & Freshwater Lake Overlooks

activities.name: Boiling Lake hike park entry
activities.tip: The official single-site eco-pass is US$8 / EC$21.74. Most travelers also hire a local guide because the trail is long, muddy, and weather-sensitive.

activities.name: Local guide for the Boiling Lake hike
activities.tip: Guide pricing commonly starts around US$50 per person and increases with transport, private guiding, or very small groups.

activities.name: Freshwater Lake viewpoint stop
Ti Kwen Glo Cho or nearby post-hike mineral pool

days.theme: Emerald Pool, Middleham Trailhead & Slow Rainforest Day

activities.name: Emerald Pool single-site visit
activities.tip: Emerald Pool is generally priced at about EC$14 for a stand-alone visit, or you can cover it with the all-sites pass if you are stacking stops.

activities.name: Middleham Falls trail access
activities.cost: ₹675 pp or included with a valid day/week pass

activities.name: Morne Trois Pitons week pass
activities.tip: The 7-day Forestry pass is officially US$50 / EC$135.85 and makes sense if you want Boiling Lake, Trafalgar, Emerald Pool, and Cabrits spread across several days.

activities.name: Rainforest lodge down-time in Laudat or Trafalgar

Portsmouth.name: Portsmouth
Portsmouth.note: Portsmouth is the right overnight stop if you want Cabrits, Purple Turtle Beach, and a more relaxed north-west base before crossing to the island's rougher northeast corner.

days.theme: Cabrits National Park, Fort Shirley & Purple Turtle Beach

activities.name: Cabrits National Park single-site pass
activities.tip: Cabrits is on the Forestry fee programme, so the same official US$8 / EC$21.74 single-site pass applies unless you already hold the day or week pass.

activities.name: Fort Shirley and hilltop cannon viewpoints
Purple Turtle Beach swim stop
Sunset at Cabrits headland or Douglas Bay
Syndicate Nature Trail / Morne Diablotin single-site pass
activities.tip: Syndicate is another Forestry-managed paid site, so the same official single-site or multi-site pass structure applies.

activities.name: Cold Soufriere and nearby village stop
Secret Beach or Douglas Bay easy coast stop
Portsmouth riverfront and town market stroll

Calibishie.name: Calibishie
Calibishie.note: Calibishie is best as a decompressing final stop: the scenery is dramatic, but services are thinner than Roseau or Portsmouth, so book dinner plans and transport ahead.

days.theme: Red Rocks, Pointe Baptiste & Village Coast

activities.name: Red Rocks and Hodges Bay viewpoint walk
Pointe Baptiste estate and sea-cliff lookouts
Calibishie village waterfront stroll
Sunset meal with ocean views

days.theme: Chaudiere Pool, Batibou Coast & Slow Northeast Morning

activities.name: Chaudiere Pool walk and swim
Batibou Beach access
Number One Beach or Woodford Hill coast stop
Return drive or minibus toward Roseau / airport side
activities.cost: ₹185–₹620 pp by bus depending on route; taxi costs rise sharply

connections.method: Shared local minibus toward Trafalgar / Laudat or a short taxi / rental-car climb into the Roseau Valley highlands (about 20–40m depending on stop)
connections.cost: ₹110–₹220 pp by bus; ₹1,400–₹1,900 taxi for up to 3; ₹4,150–₹6,650/day for a rental car

connections.method: Usually backtrack to Roseau and change to a northbound minibus, or drive the full mountain-coast route yourself (about 90–120m)
connections.cost: ₹370–₹470 pp by connecting buses; about ₹6,800–₹7,500 taxi from the Roseau side; ₹4,150–₹6,650/day for a rental car

connections.method: North-east coast transfer by shared minibus, taxi, or self-drive via villages like Bense / Woodford Hill (about 45–60m)
connections.cost: ₹185–₹250 pp by bus; about ₹8,370 taxi for up to 3; ₹4,150–₹6,650/day for a rental car

extras: Dominica's Forestry pass structure is unusually useful: the official single-site pass is US$8 / EC$21.74 (about ₹675), the all-sites day pass is US$20 / EC$54.39 (about ₹1,690), and the 7-day pass is US$50 / EC$135.85 (about ₹4,210).

Local buses are cheap but informal: they mostly leave when full from Roseau and Portsmouth, they thin out after dark, and they do not always line up well with long hikes or return trips from remote trailheads.

Rental cars give the most flexibility for Dominica's interior and northeast, but tourists also need the temporary local driving permit, usually about US$12 (roughly ₹1,000) on top of the daily rental.
If Boiling Lake is your main goal, sleep in Laudat or Trafalgar the night before and keep one weather-buffer day because heavy rain can make the trail unwise even in the drier months.
Bring water shoes or grippy sandals for Titou Gorge and spare dry bags for waterfall days; short tropical downpours still happen even in February to June.
